pure-black.$description: Pure Black — Text, image grid gutter borders, hairline dividers, nav chip outlines — the structural ink that defines the grid structure and typography

paper-white.$description: Paper White — Page canvas, nav chip backgrounds, card surface — the gallery wall behind everything

warm-charcoal.$description: Warm Charcoal — Central name card surface, secondary text, soft borders — the mid-tone that makes the pale-rose card read as a distinct layer without breaking the monochrome

blush-card.$description: Blush Card — Background of the central name card — the only chromatic accent, a dusty rose that warms the otherwise pure black-and-white system

font1.$description: font1 — detected in extracted data but not described by AI

custom-display-serif.$description: Hero name display and small print. The 238px size with line-height 0.80 and -0.04em tracking creates a tightly cropped masthead that feels printed, not rendered. Weight 300 at display size is the signature — it whispers rather than shouts, giving the name editorial weight through restraint rather than boldness. The italic treatment in the screenshot adds a calligraphic warmth that contrasts the rigid grid around it.

custom-body-serif.$description: Body copy, bio description, nav chip text, and link labels. Tight line-height 1.10 at small sizes creates a dense, catalog-like feel. The -0.025em tracking tightens the letterforms for compact, refined small text that reads as label or caption rather than prose.

font2.$description: font2 — detected in extracted data but not described by AI

ui-sans-serif.$description: ui-sans-serif — detected in extracted data but not described by AI

canvas.$description: Surface level 1: Full-viewport page background behind the image mosaic

image-tile.$description: Surface level 2: Image cells fill the grid; black serves as the gutter/border color between cells

blush-name-card.$description: Surface level 3: Central floating card with the artist's name — the single non-grayscale surface

nav-pill.$description: Surface level 4: Small pill-shaped chips in the bottom nav row
